description_long: This is a roughly hewn, irregularly shaped stone block with a weathered, pale surface. One face of the block features large, deeply incised Latin inscriptions, suggesting historical or commemorative significance. The other sides are unadorned, showing natural stone textures with some chipping and unevenness. The block appears dense and solid, with a coarse, matte finish typical of quarried stone. Its size and mass would make it a formidable obstacle in an urban environment, likely used as a barrier or remnant of an architectural structure. The overall appearance suggests age and exposure to the elements, with some surface smoothing and minor edge damage.
description: A large, pale, inscribed stone block with rough, weathered surfaces and deep Latin carvings on one face. The other sides are unmarked and display natural stone textures.
description_view_0: The front face displays prominent, deeply carved Latin inscriptions on a pale, rough surface.
description_view_1: This side shows the block's irregular, unmarked stone surface with visible chipping and a more angular profile.
description_view_2: A plain, elongated side with no inscriptions, highlighting the stone's natural, uneven texture.
description_view_3: The top or rear surface is rough and sloped, with visible tool marks or natural striations but no carvings.
attribute_location_context: ancient urban plaza or historical monument site
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or set directly on compacted earth or stone
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface shows moderate weathering with some smoothing and chipped edges, likely from prolonged outdoor exposure.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Visible Latin inscriptions deeply carved into one face.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Appears isolated but may have once formed part of a larger inscribed wall or monument.
